user_query:
I need a reproducible workflow that generates a LaTeX PDF report summarizing metadata for several LaTeX packages I use, and cleans up some rough notes. Please implement code and a shell script that does the following end-to-end:

Inputs:
- Read package names from input/packages.txt (one per line).
- Read rough notes from input/changelog_notes.txt.

Internet fetch and extraction:
- For each package in input/packages.txt, download its official page from the CTAN site (ctan.org domain), using either the package HTML page or the CTAN package JSON endpoint if available. Do not use third-party sources.
- Save the raw downloaded content per package under output/raw/ with consistent filenames ending in .html or .json as appropriate.
- Extract and normalize the following fields into one CSV row per package: package (as in input), source_type ("html" or "json"), retrieved_at (UTC ISO 8601), http_status (HTTP status code), name, version, license, description, description_length (character count of description). If a field is unavailable, leave it empty but keep the row.

Statistics:
- Compute aggregates over the CSV and write output/stats.json with at least: total_packages, success_count, failure_count, avg_description_length (rounded to 1 decimal), licenses (object with license names to counts), statuses (object mapping http_status to counts), source_types (object mapping source_type to counts), downloaded_pages_count.

Notes rewriting:
- Rewrite the content from input/changelog_notes.txt into concise, professional US English bullets. Limit to at most 8 bullets, each no more than 20 words. Save the rewritten text to output/notes_rewritten.md.

LaTeX report:
- Generate a LaTeX document that includes: (1) a table of the CSV fields for all packages; (2) a statistics section summarizing output/stats.json; (3) a "Notes" section containing output/notes_rewritten.md; (4) a reproducibility section listing the number of downloaded pages and a count of HTTP statuses observed.
- Compile the LaTeX document to output/report.pdf using a command-line LaTeX engine. Do not assume any particular engine; pick one available, and log the chosen command.

Command execution and error analysis:
- Provide run.sh that orchestrates the workflow: fetch, extract, compute stats, build LaTeX, and compile. Log all command output to output/build.log.
- If compilation or fetch fails, parse output/build.log to produce output/errors.txt containing a concise summary: exit code, any lines with "error" or "warning", and counts of each. The workflow must still produce output/stats.json and output/ctan_metadata.csv even if compilation fails.

Outputs (must exist after a successful run):
- output/raw/ (directory with per-package HTML/JSON files)
- output/ctan_metadata.csv (one row per package with the specified columns)
- output/stats.json (aggregates as specified)
- output/notes_rewritten.md (US English concise bullets)
- output/report.pdf (LaTeX-compiled PDF)
- output/build.log (full log of commands run)
- output/errors.txt (only if any errors/warnings were detected during fetch or compile)

Constraints:
- The workflow must be deterministic for a given input/packages.txt, aside from timestamps. Use only the official CTAN site (ctan.org domain) for downloads.
- Make the README.md with usage instructions (how to run run.sh and what outputs to expect).

Deliverables:
- scripts for fetching/parsing and building the report,
- run.sh,
- the generated outputs under output/ as described.

Please write everything in US English. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
